messages.content: Where can I find the Act and Statutes of VIT-AP University?
The Act and Statutes are available on the university website via the public self-disclosure links.
How do I access the official university statutes?
The Act and Statutes are available on the university website via the public self-disclosure links.
Is there a link to read the VIT-AP Act and Statutes?
The Act and Statutes are available on the university website via the public self-disclosure links.
Where are the foundational Act and Statutes of the university published?
The Act and Statutes are available on the university website via the public self-disclosure links.
What is the university's official policy on fee refunds?
If a candidate withdraws from the programme, the refund of fees will be made as per the norms of the UGC.
How does the fee refund process work if I withdraw?
If a candidate withdraws from the programme, the refund of fees will be made as per the norms of the UGC.
Will I get my money back if I cancel my admission?
If a candidate withdraws from the programme, the refund of fees will be made as per the norms of the UGC.
Are fee refunds governed by any specific guidelines?
If a candidate withdraws from the programme, the refund of fees will be made as per the norms of the UGC.
Does the university have facilities for differently-abled students?
Yes, the university provides a barrier-free environment and specific facilities for differently-abled individuals.
Are there accommodations for handicapped or differently-abled people?
Yes, the university provides a barrier-free environment and specific facilities for differently-abled individuals.
Is the campus accessible for students with disabilities?
Yes, the university provides a barrier-free environment and specific facilities for differently-abled individuals.
What does the university's vision statement say?
The vision is to transform higher education and contribute to the improvement of life itself by application of knowledge.
What is the official vision of VIT-AP?
The vision is to transform higher education and contribute to the improvement of life itself by application of knowledge.
Can you tell me the university's vision?
The vision is to transform higher education and contribute to the improvement of life itself by application of knowledge.
What is the university motto?
The motto of the university is 'Apply Knowledge, Improve Life!'
What catchphrase or motto does VIT-AP use?
The motto of the university is 'Apply Knowledge, Improve Life!'
What is the official university motto?
The motto of the university is 'Apply Knowledge, Improve Life!'
What are the core values of VIT-AP?
Core values include a Student-Centered Environment, Transformative Leadership, Teamwork, Striving for Excellence, Respect and Inclusion, Sustainability, Creativity, Accountability, and Integrity.
Which principles form the core values of the institution?
Core values include a Student-Centered Environment, Transformative Leadership, Teamwork, Striving for Excellence, Respect and Inclusion, Sustainability, Creativity, Accountability, and Integrity.
What ethics and values does the university promote?
Core values include a Student-Centered Environment, Transformative Leadership, Teamwork, Striving for Excellence, Respect and Inclusion, Sustainability, Creativity, Accountability, and Integrity.
Does the university have an Equal Opportunity Cell?
Yes, the university maintains an Equal Opportunity Cell and a Socio-Economically Disadvantaged Group Cell (SEDG).
Is there a dedicated cell for socio-economically disadvantaged groups?
Yes, the university maintains an Equal Opportunity Cell and a Socio-Economically Disadvantaged Group Cell (SEDG).
What administrative body supports marginalized students?
Yes, the university maintains an Equal Opportunity Cell and a Socio-Economically Disadvantaged Group Cell (SEDG).
What is the curfew time for hostellers?
On class days, all hostellers must be inside the hostel by 7:30 PM.
By what time do hostel students need to be inside?
On class days, all hostellers must be inside the hostel by 7:30 PM.
What is the attendance timing for hostellers?
On class days, all hostellers must be inside the hostel by 7:30 PM.
When should hostel students return on working days?
On class days, all hostellers must be inside the hostel by 7:30 PM.
Are hostel students allowed to stay with local guardians?
A hosteller may avail leave and go to their approved local guardian's place with proper prior intimation to the authorities.
Can a hosteller visit their local guardian for the weekend?
A hosteller may avail leave and go to their approved local guardian's place with proper prior intimation to the authorities.
What is the leave policy for visiting a local guardian?
A hosteller may avail leave and go to their approved local guardian's place with proper prior intimation to the authorities.
What value stamp paper is needed for the student and hostel affidavit?
The affidavit must be signed separately by the student, parent, and hostel on a Rs. 20/- Non-Judicial Stamp Paper and notarized.
What is the required stamp paper denomination for admission affidavits?
The affidavit must be signed separately by the student, parent, and hostel on a Rs. 20/- Non-Judicial Stamp Paper and notarized.
Do I need a Rs. 20 stamp paper for the hostel affidavit?
The affidavit must be signed separately by the student, parent, and hostel on a Rs. 20/- Non-Judicial Stamp Paper and notarized.
Is a medical certificate required for admission to the hostel?
Yes, students must submit a Certificate of Physical Fitness certified by an authorized physician.
Do I need to prove my physical fitness before joining?
Yes, students must submit a Certificate of Physical Fitness certified by an authorized physician.
What health documents are mandatory for admission?
Yes, students must submit a Certificate of Physical Fitness certified by an authorized physician.
How are B.Tech admissions decided?
Admission is based purely on the ranks secured in the VITEEE Computer Based Test.
What is the main criteria for getting into B.Tech?
Admission is based purely on the ranks secured in the VITEEE Computer Based Test.
Does the university use 12th marks for B.Tech admission?
Admission is based purely on the ranks secured in the VITEEE Computer Based Test.
How does VIT equate different VITEEE exam forms?
The statistical procedure of Equipercentile Equating is used to adjust scores and identify equivalent percentile ranks.
What statistical method normalizes the VITEEE scores?
The statistical procedure of Equipercentile Equating is used to adjust scores and identify equivalent percentile ranks.
Is the VITEEE scoring normalized across sessions?
The statistical procedure of Equipercentile Equating is used to adjust scores and identify equivalent percentile ranks.
Is there negative marking in the VIT entrance exams?
No, there are no negative marks for wrong answers.
Will I lose marks for guessing incorrectly on the VITEEE?
No, there are no negative marks for wrong answers.
Does the VITMEE have a penalty for wrong answers?
No, there are no negative marks for wrong answers.
What is the deadline to submit final UG documents for M.Sc. admission?
The documents should be submitted before 15 August 2026, failing which admission will be cancelled.
By what date must PG students submit their provisional degree certificate?
The documents should be submitted before 15 August 2026, failing which admission will be cancelled.
What happens if I don't submit my final M.Sc. documents by August 15?
The documents should be submitted before 15 August 2026, failing which admission will be cancelled.
What is the eligibility for the Direct Ph.D. program?
Graduates must have an outstanding academic record with a minimum of 75% marks or a CGPA of 7.5 on a 10-point scale in their Bachelor's degree.
Can I do a Ph.D. directly after my B.Tech?
Graduates must have an outstanding academic record with a minimum of 75% marks or a CGPA of 7.5 on a 10-point scale in their Bachelor's degree.
What CGPA is required for Direct Ph.D. admission?
Graduates must have an outstanding academic record with a minimum of 75% marks or a CGPA of 7.5 on a 10-point scale in their Bachelor's degree.
What is the stipend for a GATE-qualified Chancellor Fellow in the second year?
A GATE-qualified Chancellor Fellow receives ₹ 50,000 per month in their second and third years.
How much does a 2nd year GATE-qualified Ph.D. student get paid?
A GATE-qualified Chancellor Fellow receives ₹ 50,000 per month in their second and third years.
What is the Chancellor Fellowship stipend amount?
A GATE-qualified Chancellor Fellow receives ₹ 50,000 per month in their second and third years.
Do SC/ST students need a specific certificate for admission?
Yes, they must provide a Community Certificate recognized under the Constitution and signed by a competent authority.
What caste document is required for SC/ST quotas?
Yes, they must provide a Community Certificate recognized under the Constitution and signed by a competent authority.
Is a community certificate mandatory for scheduled castes?
Yes, they must provide a Community Certificate recognized under the Constitution and signed by a competent authority.
What constitutes sexual harassment according to the university?
Sexual misconduct includes unwanted physical contact, persistent unwelcome comments, and sending obscene messages or pictures on social media.
How does the code of ethics define sexual misconduct?
Sexual misconduct includes unwanted physical contact, persistent unwelcome comments, and sending obscene messages or pictures on social media.
Are inappropriate social media messages considered sexual harassment?
Sexual misconduct includes unwanted physical contact, persistent unwelcome comments, and sending obscene messages or pictures on social media.
What is the penalty for ragging?
Ragging is an illegal activity liable for imprisonment for two years with a fine up to Rs. 10,000, and expulsion from the University.
Can a student be expelled for ragging?
Ragging is an illegal activity liable for imprisonment for two years with a fine up to Rs. 10,000, and expulsion from the University.
What legal consequences face students who participate in ragging?
Ragging is an illegal activity liable for imprisonment for two years with a fine up to Rs. 10,000, and expulsion from the University.
Are mobile phones allowed during exams?
No, possession of cell phones or any electronic devices inside the examination hall is strictly prohibited.
Can I keep my phone in my pocket during an examination?
No, possession of cell phones or any electronic devices inside the examination hall is strictly prohibited.
Is carrying an electronic device into the exam hall a violation?
No, possession of cell phones or any electronic devices inside the examination hall is strictly prohibited.
Who do I appeal to if I receive a disciplinary punishment?
An appeal against any order of punishment can be submitted to the Registrar of the University.
Where can I file an appeal against an ethics violation ruling?
An appeal against any order of punishment can be submitted to the Registrar of the University.
Who handles appeals for disciplinary actions?
An appeal against any order of punishment can be submitted to the Registrar of the University.
Who holds the ownership of patents filed at the university?
VIT-AP University acts as the sole owner of all Intellectual Property Rights generated using its resources.
Does the inventor own the patent or does the university?
VIT-AP University acts as the sole owner of all Intellectual Property Rights generated using its resources.
Who is the legal owner of IPR generated on campus?
VIT-AP University acts as the sole owner of all Intellectual Property Rights generated using its resources.
Who pays for the patent attorney and filing fees?
All expenses, including statutory fees and patent attorney fees, are completely borne by the university's IPR Cell.
Do students have to pay out of pocket to file a patent?
All expenses, including statutory fees and patent attorney fees, are completely borne by the university's IPR Cell.
Who covers the financial cost of intellectual property registration?
All expenses, including statutory fees and patent attorney fees, are completely borne by the university's IPR Cell.
What is the revenue sharing ratio for commercialized patents?
The revenue generated from commercial exploitation of IP is shared in a 60:40 ratio between the inventor(s) and the University.
How much money does the inventor get if a patent is sold?
The revenue generated from commercial exploitation of IP is shared in a 60:40 ratio between the inventor(s) and the University.
What percentage of IP royalties goes to the creator?
The revenue generated from commercial exploitation of IP is shared in a 60:40 ratio between the inventor(s) and the University.
What is the timeline for returning confidential information under an NDA?
The receiving party must return all Confidential Information within 5 days of a written demand by the disclosing party.
How many days do I have to return NDA documents?
The receiving party must return all Confidential Information within 5 days of a written demand by the disclosing party.
What is the timeframe to surrender confidential info upon request?
The receiving party must return all Confidential Information within 5 days of a written demand by the disclosing party.
What image views are required for a design patent application?
Seven specific views in JPEG format: front, rear, left, right, top, bottom, and a top perspective view.
How many photographs do I need for design registration?
Seven specific views in JPEG format: front, rear, left, right, top, bottom, and a top perspective view.
What format should the design invention photos be in?
Seven specific views in JPEG format: front, rear, left, right, top, bottom, and a top perspective view.
What marks are required for IT sector placements?
IT sector placements require a minimum of 60% marks in undergraduate programs and in classes X and XII.
What is the minimum percentage to sit for IT placements?
IT sector placements require a minimum of 60% marks in undergraduate programs and in classes X and XII.
Do I need 60% in 10th and 12th for campus placements?
IT sector placements require a minimum of 60% marks in undergraduate programs and in classes X and XII.
Is a gap year allowed for placements?
Yes, an academic gap of a maximum of two years is permitted for placements.
Can I sit for placements if I have a 1-year academic gap?
Yes, an academic gap of a maximum of two years is permitted for placements.
What is the maximum allowed academic gap for IT placements?
Yes, an academic gap of a maximum of two years is permitted for placements.
What is the STARS scheme?
Support The Advancement of Rural Students (STARS) offers a 100% scholarship, free hostel, and food for rural AP government junior college toppers.
Does the university offer full scholarships for rural students?
Support The Advancement of Rural Students (STARS) offers a 100% scholarship, free hostel, and food for rural AP government junior college toppers.
Who is eligible for the STARS program?
Support The Advancement of Rural Students (STARS) offers a 100% scholarship, free hostel, and food for rural AP government junior college toppers.
How does the university teach entrepreneurship?
In partnership with the Wadhwani Foundation, over 7500 students are trained annually in idea validation and business venture formation.
Are there startup training courses for students?
In partnership with the Wadhwani Foundation, over 7500 students are trained annually in idea validation and business venture formation.
Who helps the university conduct entrepreneurship training?
In partnership with the Wadhwani Foundation, over 7500 students are trained annually in idea validation and business venture formation.
What is the role of VTBIF?
The VITAP TBI Foundation (VTBIF) is a Section-8 company that supports tech startups through incubation and product development.
Which entity handles business incubation on campus?
The VITAP TBI Foundation (VTBIF) is a Section-8 company that supports tech startups through incubation and product development.
What does the university's startup incubator do?
The VITAP TBI Foundation (VTBIF) is a Section-8 company that supports tech startups through incubation and product development.
What is the university's target Faculty-Student Ratio?
The Institutional Development Plan targets achieving a Faculty-Student Ratio (FSR) of 1:15.
What FSR does the university aim for?
The Institutional Development Plan targets achieving a Faculty-Student Ratio (FSR) of 1:15.
How many students per faculty member is the university targeting?
The Institutional Development Plan targets achieving a Faculty-Student Ratio (FSR) of 1:15.
What is the V-LAUNCH program?
V-LAUNCH provides internal seed funding (up to Rs. 2.00 Lakhs) for a faculty member to collaborate with students to develop product prototypes.
How much funding does a V-LAUNCH project receive?
V-LAUNCH provides internal seed funding (up to Rs. 2.00 Lakhs) for a faculty member to collaborate with students to develop product prototypes.
What scheme gives seed money for faculty-student prototypes?
V-LAUNCH provides internal seed funding (up to Rs. 2.00 Lakhs) for a faculty member to collaborate with students to develop product prototypes.
What is the target for external research funding?
The university aims to fetch external research funding of at least 10 crores, or 2 crores per year.
How much external funding does the university want to generate annually?
The university aims to fetch external research funding of at least 10 crores, or 2 crores per year.
What are the financial goals for research grants?
The university aims to fetch external research funding of at least 10 crores, or 2 crores per year.
How many research papers should a faculty member publish?
The university targets an average of 4 publications per faculty member.
What is the publication quota or target for professors?
The university targets an average of 4 publications per faculty member.
What is the 5-year goal for faculty research output?
The university targets an average of 4 publications per faculty member.
Are there plans to build houses for faculty on campus?
Yes, the IDP includes action points to construct on-campus residential facilities for the faculty.
Will faculty get accommodation inside the university?
Yes, the IDP includes action points to construct on-campus residential facilities for the faculty.
Does the infrastructure plan include faculty quarters?
Yes, the IDP includes action points to construct on-campus residential facilities for the faculty.
What is the DYOD program?
'Design Your Own Degree' (DYOD) allows students to customize their academic path by choosing minors, specializations, and double majors.
What does DYOD stand for in academics?
'Design Your Own Degree' (DYOD) allows students to customize their academic path by choosing minors, specializations, and double majors.
How does the university allow students to customize degrees?
'Design Your Own Degree' (DYOD) allows students to customize their academic path by choosing minors, specializations, and double majors.
Can I take a double major?
'Design Your Own Degree' (DYOD) allows students to customize their academic path by choosing minors, specializations, and double majors.
What is the Engineering Clinics course?
It is a hands-on, project-based learning course where first and second-year students collaborate to build working prototypes.
How do students get hands-on prototype experience?
It is a hands-on, project-based learning course where first and second-year students collaborate to build working prototypes.
What happens in the Engineering Clinics?
It is a hands-on, project-based learning course where first and second-year students collaborate to build working prototypes.
Does the curriculum involve industry experts?
Yes, the 8th module of every subject is taught by visiting industry experts to connect classroom concepts to real-world practices.
Are guest lectures integrated into the syllabus?
Yes, the 8th module of every subject is taught by visiting industry experts to connect classroom concepts to real-world practices.
Who teaches the 8th module of academic subjects?
Yes, the 8th module of every subject is taught by visiting industry experts to connect classroom concepts to real-world practices.
What is the question paper code for a Ph.D. in Computer Science?
The question paper code for Computer Science and Engineering is CIE.
If I apply for a Ph.D. in CSE, what is the exam code?
The question paper code for Computer Science and Engineering is CIE.
What exam code should I use for a Ph.D. in Information Technology?
The question paper code for Computer Science and Engineering is CIE.
What are the core research domains for the Deep Tech Fellowship?
Domains include Generative AI, Quantum Computing, Blockchain, Space Technologies, and Precision Agriculture.
Which fields qualify for the Chancellor's Deep Tech Fellowship?
Domains include Generative AI, Quantum Computing, Blockchain, Space Technologies, and Precision Agriculture.
What topics does the Deep Tech Ph.D. focus on?
Domains include Generative AI, Quantum Computing, Blockchain, Space Technologies, and Precision Agriculture.
What is the presentation mode for Paper 475 'Novel Federated Edge Learning'?
Paper 475 is presented ONLINE in Track 1D at the AISP conference.
Is the federated edge learning paper presented online or offline?
Paper 475 is presented ONLINE in Track 1D at the AISP conference.
Which track hosts paper 475 online?
Paper 475 is presented ONLINE in Track 1D at the AISP conference.
What is the title of Paper ID 309?
The paper is titled 'Vehicle Tracking and License Plate Recognition Using YOLOv11 Integrated with OCR'.
Which paper covers vehicle tracking using YOLOv11?
The paper is titled 'Vehicle Tracking and License Plate Recognition Using YOLOv11 Integrated with OCR'.
What topic does Paper 309 address at the conference?
The paper is titled 'Vehicle Tracking and License Plate Recognition Using YOLOv11 Integrated with OCR'.
